entities.name: Claude

entities.name: 设计流程 (design process)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：设计师过去被教导的发散-收敛等传统流程。这套被当作福音的流程现在已经死了，被极速的工程执行力倒逼改变，要么贴身支持执行，要么制定短期愿景。

evidence.quote: This design process that designers have been taught, we sort of treat it as gospel.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: How is the design process changing with the rise of AI?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so I think some context around this, is I did a talk at a conference in Berlin a few months ago in September, and I called it Don't Trust the Design Process, where I basically just said, "Hey, you know this design process that designers have been taught, where you go and you go off and you do a bunch of research and discovery, and then you diverge, you converge, diverge, converge."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: How widespread do you think the design process shift is going to be?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And so I think it's something that people are starting to feel across the industry, where they're like, "Oh yeah, we can't do the old design process anymore.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: People clearly have invested their entire careers in learning, teaching, using this really stable design process.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Are you still doing elements of the traditional design process?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I think that's a really important part of the design process, to be able to just think about 8 to 10 different ways to do something.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Like I mentioned, the design process has changed so, so much in this past year, and I feel like I've just picked up so many hard skills that I wouldn't have necessarily had the time to do if I was just managing a team.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think at any point if I'm managing a team again, I think it will give me the empathy and understanding of how the design process has changed.

entities.name: 设计系统 (design system)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：设计师试图引导工程师参考的代码设计规范，因为 Claude 在写代码时并不总是能自动抓取设计系统里的组件。

evidence.quote: So I also just try to point engineers to our design system and stuff like that in code, because right now Claude is writing a lot of the code and it's not always picking up stuff in the design system and whatnot.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 心理安全感 (psychological safety)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：团队内部能互相吐槽、甚至取笑领导口头禅所体现出的安全感。它与高标准不冲突，就像严厉的父母，团队知道你不会随意解雇他们，但也清楚你要求做到最好。

evidence.quote: I'm not forcing it in that way or anything, but when I think about the psychological safety, and teams and people that just get along with each other, when you think about your friends, you're always willing to push the boundaries a little bit and roast them.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so the way I think about balancing this out is you have to create this baseline of psychological safety, and people feel comfortable both with each other and with you, but you also have to make sure that they know that you have really high standards.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think these two things can feel like they're at tension, but I think they work really well together, because it's like once you have that psychological safety, you have people trusting each other and you, applying the high standards actually I think becomes potentially easier because you can do it without fear, I think.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Slack
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：在 AI 实验室里如同「金矿」的内部通讯软件，充满了行业洞见、哲学辩论以及正在构建的 AI 应用的最新动态。

evidence.quote: And so a lot of it is like, yeah, our Slack is a goldmine.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think that is the best AI news is probably internally, if you're ever at one of these companies in the Slack.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I've been trying to actually use Claude Code more remotely, through both mobile and through Slack as well.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think when I mentioned going through Slack and looking at all the stuff that people are making, that's kind of what I'm doing.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Socratica
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：一个学校发起的构建实际东西并像科学项目一样展示的运动，代表了那种乐于尝试构建并分享的社区。

evidence.quote: I think my one call it here too, is I went to a school that started something called Socratica a few years after, actually a while after I graduated.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0
